title: Papaver bivalve
taxonomy: Papaver bivalve, also known as the two-lobed poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The species is classified within the genus Papaver and is closely related to other poppy species. Papaver bivalve is further classified within the tribe Papavereae, which includes various poppy species and their hybrids. The exact classification of Papaver bivalve may vary slightly depending on the source and current botanical consensus.
uses: Papaver bivalve is a species of poppy known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. Its roots and rhizomes are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and potential side effects. In some traditional remedies, Papaver bivalve is used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis.

intro: Papaver bivalve, also known as silver poppy or red poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its delicate, papery flowers and unique seed pods.
